predictions.source_quote: Our projection is that core PCE will hit 2% year over year by the second quarter of this year.
We are expecting inflation on average to be about 2% over the course of this year.
We're expecting it to happen even faster and even dip below the Fed's 2% inflation target a bit over 2025 to 2027.

predictions.variable: Fed funds rate target range year-end
predictions.source_quote: In the near term, our views are expecting a 3.75% to 4.00% target range at the end of 2024.
We're expecting our first cut in March, which is now the market consensus.
We expect the Fed to cut starting this year and proceed aggressively over the course of this year heading into 2025, wrapping up cuts by 2026.
That lower federal-funds rate will drive the 10-year Treasury yield ultimately down to our long-term projection of 2.75%, down from current levels of 4.0%.
Including a fall in the 30-year mortgage rate, which we expect to ultimately fall to 4.25%, just a bit above prepandemic levels.

predictions.variable: Investment-grade credit spread observation (not a prediction)
predictions.source_quote: Investment grade has tightened 18 basis points. We're sub-100 spread in investment-grade today.
predictions.reason: Observation about current state, not a forward prediction

predictions.variable: High-yield credit spread observation (not a prediction)
predictions.reason: Observation about current state, not a forward prediction

predictions.source_quote: In the fourth quarter, we expect 1.7% real GDP growth quarter over quarter.

predictions.variable: Cumulative real GDP growth vs consensus (5-year)
predictions.source_quote: On a five-year time horizon we do expect a cumulative 3% more real GDP growth than consensus, owing to principally our views on the supply side of the economy in terms of labor force expansion and also productivity growth.
The slowdown in GDP growth that we're expecting for 2024 will generate a modest uptick in unemployment averaging 4.2% that year.
We think unemployment will peak at about 4.5% in the fourth quarter of 2024.

predictions.variable: S&P 500 price/fair value ratio observation (not a prediction)
predictions.source_quote: Our price/fair value metric, it was at 1 at Dec. 21.
predictions.reason: Observation about current valuation, not a forward prediction

predictions.variable: Value stocks discount to fair value
predictions.source_quote: Value stocks are still trading at a pretty respectable discount from fair value, trading at about a 10% discount.
